sections.title: Passage 1: The History of the Bicycle
sections.instructions: Read the passage and answer Questions 1-5.
sections.content: The bicycle was invented in the 19th century and has since become one of the most common forms of transportation globally. Early models, such as the Penny Farthing, featured a massive front wheel and a tiny rear wheel, making them difficult and dangerous to ride. It wasn't until the development of the safety bicycle in the late 1880s, which introduced wheels of equal size and a chain drive, that cycling became widely accessible.

questions.text: The Penny Farthing was easy to ride.
The development of the ______ made cycling widely accessible.

sections.title: Passage 2: Urban Agriculture
sections.instructions: Read the passage and answer Questions 4-6.
sections.content: Urban agriculture involves growing plants and raising animals within and around cities. It provides a significant amount of food to urban populations and reduces the carbon footprint associated with transporting food from rural areas. Rooftop gardens and vertical farms are increasingly common in modern metropolises.

questions.text: Urban agriculture only involves growing plants.
Where are rooftop gardens commonly found today?
